headlines.title: Fake Trump and Oprah ads fuel a wave of Medicare scams on Facebook, report says
headlines.description: Meta, the parent of Facebook and Instagram, is failing to stop scammers who repeatedly buy ads on the platforms to target seniors with scams, a new report says.

headlines.title: Inside China’s race to dominate humanoid robotics industry
headlines.description: Beijing has put the robotics industry front and center of its national agenda as the tech race with Washington heats up in several key areas, including AI.

headlines.title: AI-generated pro-Spencer Pratt mayoral campaign videos point to a new political reality
headlines.description: Spencer Pratt’s bid for Los Angeles mayor gained viral traction after AI videos, which Pratt said were “fan-made,” circulated across social media.

headlines.title: Musk’s attorney apologizes for his absence at trial during closing arguments
headlines.description: Elon Musk had traveled to China with President Donald Trump even though the judge said he wasn’t excused.

headlines.title: A judge told Musk he wasn’t excused from trial. He went to China with Trump anyway.
headlines.description: Elon Musk traveled to China with President Donald Trump, despite the trial over his lawsuit with OpenAI and a judge’s order that he remain ready to be recalled.

headlines.title: Most U.S. doctors are quietly using this AI tool. Few patients know about it.
headlines.description: OpenEvidence, an AI-powered medical search tool, has become a fast friend to America’s doctors and is now used by nearly two-thirds of physicians.

headlines.title: AI chipmaker Cerebras soars 90% in year’s biggest IPO so far
headlines.description: Artificial intelligence chipmaker Cerebras surged 90% in its trading debut, setting the bar for the biggest initial public stock offering of the year so far.

headlines.title: Authorities say she asked ChatGPT about mixing alcohol and meds. Then, two men turned up dead.
headlines.description: Kim So-young is accused of asking ChatGPT for lethal dosing advice before giving three men benzodiazepine-laced drinks. Two died.

headlines.title: Supreme Court rejects Virginia Democrats’ attempt to revive new congressional map
headlines.description: The decision leaves in place a Virginia Supreme Court ruling that concluded the process leading up to a referendum approving a new congressional map was flawed.

headlines.title: DOJ pushing to indict Raúl Castro over 1996 downing of civilian planes
headlines.description: According to two U.S. officials, the Justice Department is pushing to indict former Cuban president Raúl Castro over the deaths of four Cuban Americans in the 1996 downing of civilian planes. NBC News' Ryan Reilly breaks down the state of relations between the U.S. and Cuba.

headlines.title: Man accused of plotting terror attack on New York City synagogue arrested
headlines.description: A man suspected of plotting a terror attack on a synagogue in New York City has been arrested. NBC News' Tom Winter has details on the arrest and the suspect's alleged involvement with a group tied to Hezbollah.

headlines.title: Trump Wraps China Summit With Xi Jinping: What Are the Results?
headlines.description: President Donald Trump is concluding his high-stakes visit to China and is already declaring it a success. The summit’s results so far are still unclear with no announcements of any new trade deals or purchases of American products. Trump suggested that airplane maker Boeing struck a deal to sell more aircrafts to Chinese companies, but offered few details. NBC’s Garrett Haake reports and moderator of Meet the Press Kristin Welker joins TODAY with analysis.
